title: Cathartocarpus moschatus
taxonomy: Cathartocarpus moschatus is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its flowers and seed pods. The family Fabaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, with over 19,000 known species. Cathartocarpus moschatus is also classified within the tribe Phaseoleae and the subfamily Faboideae. The exact classification of Cathartocarpus moschatus is still under review and may be subject to change as new research becomes available.

intro: Cathartocarpus moschatus, also known as the musky heart or heart of the woods, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. The health benefits of Cathartocarpus moschatus include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
